share_basis_note: ~37.76M diluted shares (incl. Nov-2025 tangible-equity-unit dilution) x $156.65 (2026-07-10 last close, last <= as_of) = ~$5.915B. Price from Yahoo chart API [S005], cross-checked vs stockanalysis [S006] (both $156.65 to the cent) per freshness.json. 10-K cover shows 35.68M basic (Feb-16-2026); Q4'25 diluted 38.7M; difference = TEU dilution.

module_signals.finding: Segment revenue, end-market mix, financials, balance sheet, Q1'26 momentum, and price all primary/verified (FY2025 10-K/PR + Q1'26 call + 2-source price). Key gap: company discloses NO discrete robotics revenue line (O1) -> the headline batch metric is an ESTIMATE, not a fact. Operator/proxy OPEN (O3). EDGAR direct 403 -> used faithful relays. Completeness ~60% = haircut, not veto.

Real secular vectors (minimally-invasive/robotic surgery, industrial automation, optional humanoid) but Novanta is a picks-and-shovels precision-component supplier, not a chokepoint. Robotics is ~15-25% of revenue on a components-into-robots basis (ESTIMATE); humanoid/embodied-AI specifically <1-2% and prototype-stage (Holoscan, meaningful 2027 per mgmt). Base = medical 53% + advanced-industrial 47%. Demand real but single-digit (robotics rev +7%, organic +3%); the +50% robotics bookings is a forward signal partly inflated by full-year orders pulled into Q1.

Genuine designed-in / switching-cost moat: ~44% gross margin, vitality index 27%, safety-certified (only servo drive in NVIDIA Holoscan lab), medical consumables 15% of revenue (recurring). But component-level, not NVDA-class bottleneck; competitors per niche; serial-acquirer model -> GAAP<<adjusted, thin FCF. Operator grade provisional 3.5/5 (net-cash discipline; M&A-dependent). Durable but cyclical -> ceiling capped below Core.

High-margin, net-cash (~$121M) fortress balance sheet; survives a downturn (no solvency risk). BUT cash conversion is the soft spot: FY2025 FCF only $54M (yield ~0.9%), GAAP EPS $1.47 vs adj $3.29 (~2.2x gap from acquisition amortization). Owner earnings run below adjusted EPS -> valuing on adjusted flatters. FY2026 OCF guide $145-185M is a large step-up that must be proven (a confirmation item, not yet cash).

No thesis breakage or solvency risk at as-of (net cash, profitable, sticky). The dominant risk is a 'paying-up trap': ~47x adj EPS on a mid-single-digit organic grower -> multiple compression toward $100-120 (near 52wk low $98.27) loses 30-35% with NO thesis break. Secondary: bookings pull-forward reversing, humanoid option never converting, industrial cyclicality. These are SIZE/price controls + kill monitors, not a hard veto.

At $156.65 (~$5.915B): ~47.6x trailing adj EPS, ~23x fwd EV/EBITDA, FCF yield <1%, price -8.8% vs 52-week high. 3-scenario 5y IRR: bear -8.5% / base +1.2% / bull +8.4%. Base is FAR below the 8% hurdle and even the bull only just clears it AND requires the 2027 humanoid option to convert. 8%-hurdle-neutral entry ~$108-115 (near the 52wk low). PRICE IS THE BINDING CONSTRAINT: no margin of safety, and the robotics reason to pay up is mostly anticipation.

capital_cycle.supply_barrier: med-high for Novanta's incumbent designed-in niche; low for the hyped humanoid motion-control land-grab
capital_cycle.read: Judged on the robotics segment: the humanoid/embodied-AI opportunity is a visible, consensus, capital-attracting theme -> naive-guard says do NOT pay up for it. We do NOT know when/if humanoid revenue inflects (mgmt says 2027). Novanta's durable part is the incumbent precision-motion/end-of-arm niche (real switching cost); the fragile part is the humanoid premium the market may be pricing in.
capital_cycle.caution: EARLY-ANTICIPATION (ceiling on enthusiasm, not a verdict flip): keep the humanoid option discounted; the industrial base is mid-cycle (not PEAK).

starter_zone: No new money at $156.65. A 3-5% STARTER opens on EITHER a de-rate into ~$100-120 (base 5y IRR back >= 8% hurdle) OR robotics converting from bookings to sustained double-digit REVENUE with book-to-bill >1.1 for 2+ quarters (pull-forward disproven) + FY26 OCF landing in guide.
add_zone: Scale toward 8-12% only into ~$95-105 (toward/through the 52wk low) AND/OR on confirmed durable double-digit organic + a dated, dollar-quantified Holoscan/humanoid ramp. Cyclicality caps the ceiling below Core.
trim_or_no_chase_zone: Current $156.65 is already above the ~$115 no-margin-of-safety line -> no chase; hard no-chase toward the $171.85 ATH. (No existing position; if held, HOLD a quality compounder, do not add new money here.)
binding_constraint: PRICE (no margin of safety). At $156.65 the base 5y IRR (+1.2%) is far below the 8% hurdle, the stock is -8.8% off its ATH, FCF yield <1%, and the robotics/humanoid upside that justifies the ~47x multiple is still anticipation (2027), not cash. Completeness ~60% (no discrete robotics disclosure O1, operator OPEN O3) independently caps the ceiling at the WATCH/STARTER boundary; price pulls it to WATCH.
kill_criteria: Two-plus quarters of organic revenue flat/negative AND book-to-bill <1.0 (demand narrative false).
Robotics & Automation revenue decelerates to low-single-digit/negative while bookings normalize (Q1 pull-forward confirmed, not durable demand).
Gross margin structurally breaks below ~42% (pricing/mix erosion).
Goodwill impairment or a value-destroying large acquisition (M&A discipline lost).
Holoscan/humanoid content commoditized or designed-out (option -> zero) -> loses the premium.
Price stays above ~$115 with no offsetting growth acceleration -> remains WATCH/no-chase (the case today at $156.65).
runner_dissent: The bull instinct: a net-cash (~$121M), 44%-gross-margin, designed-in specialty-components compounder with surging robotics bookings (+50%), a 15%-of-revenue recurring consumables franchise, AND the only servo drive in NVIDIA's Holoscan safety lab -- why not a small starter on the robotics option? The framework overrides to WATCH because PRICE is the constraint: ~47x adjusted EPS on a mid-single-digit organic grower gives base 5y IRR +1.2% (<<8%), and even the +8.4% bull requires the 2027 humanoid option to actually convert -- i.e. you would be paying up TODAY for anticipation. The robotics business is real but modest (~15-25% of revenue, mostly industrial, single-digit revenue growth) and humanoid/embodied-AI is <1-2% and pre-revenue. On the batch's own test (demand exploded + bottleneck + already cash), NOVT fails the bottleneck leg (good component moat, not a chokepoint) and only half-passes the cash leg. It is a quality WATCH, not the robotics NVDA. Not-fully-resolved OPEN items: discrete robotics revenue $ (O1, company doesn't disclose), operator/proxy (O3), owner-earnings bridge quality (O4), 2027 humanoid magnitude (O5).
